relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Paul and Timothy address Corinth and all Achaia from calling.
Grace and peace open the letter’s healing field.
The Divine is Father of compassions and Source of all comfort.
Comfort received in pressure becomes comfort given to others.
Messiah’s sufferings and Messiah’s comfort both overflow.
Paul’s pressure and comfort both serve their endurance and saving/healing.
Shared suffering becomes shared comfort.
Asia’s pressure was beyond strength, even into despair of life.
The death-sentence within teaches trust in the Divine who raises dead ones.
Past rescue, present rescue, and future rescue gather in hope.
Many prayers become many faces of thanksgiving.
Conscience witnesses sincerity and Divine grace rather than fleshly wisdom.
Paul wants transparent reading and understanding.
Mutual boasting belongs to the day of the Lord Jesus.
His travel plan intended a second grace.
Corinth was meant to participate in the journey onward.
Paul denies planning from fleshly instability.
Their word is not Yes and No.
In Messiah Jesus, the Divine Yes has come to be.
Every Divine promise finds Yes in him and Amen through him.
The Divine confirms and anoints the shared body in Messiah.
Spirit is seal and pledge in the heart.
Paul says his delay was to spare them.
True leadership does not lord over trust; it works with joy.

contemplative_note: 2 Corinthians 1 turns suffering into transmissible comfort. The one who has been held in pressure becomes able to hold others, not by dominance but as coworker of joy.
